title: Feed Discovery, WHATWG URL Identity, Redirects, and SSRF · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent university archive receives a different publisher mix, unknown XML shape, validator state, tenant, async schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers feed discovery, whatwg url identity, redirects, and ssrf into a changed authorized TypeScript discovery policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of authorized TypeScript discovery policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Distinguish direct feeds from bounded HTML autodiscovery and require explicit source intent and supported media evidence.
Admit supported alternate RSS or Atom links, resolve against the final document URL, deduplicate, rank transparently, and bound candidates.
Use WHATWG URL parsing and a documented equivalence policy without collapsing query, case, port, path, fragment, or percent-encoding distinctions unsafely.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Direct Discovery Mode
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 3026 during a cross-tenant privacy and cache audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile direct and autodiscovery, URL identity, redirects, SSRF, and provenance; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one PostgreSQL constraint, transaction, migration, upsert, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Direct Discovery Mode.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish direct feeds from bounded html autodiscovery and require explicit source intent and supported media evidence. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Direct Discovery Mode, keep Feed Ts Direct Discovery Mode connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.instruction:
Order the Feed Ts Direct Discovery Mode Feed aggregation and TypeScript source-to-reader-and-recovery evidence cycle for this lab task before implementing it.
